close_reason: Fixed in commit 8a284ca (add 30s HTTP timeout to doppler and gcp-sm clients)
description:
src/lib.rs:155 constructs reqwest::Client::new() with no timeout, no connect_timeout. Default reqwest has no per-request timeout. Network partition or unresponsive endpoint can hang get()/put() futures for the OS-level connection timeout (minutes-to-hours on Linux).

Operational impact: long-running daemons that call get() periodically would silently freeze the calling task on partition. Token rotation hourly + a partition window stretches into a stuck process.

Cross-crate aspect: every REST backend in this workspace has the same gap (verified: secretx-doppler, secretx-hashicorp-vault all use Client::new() with no timeout). Workspace-wide convention. Fixing in gcp-sm alone leaves the pattern inconsistent; fixing in all REST backends is the right scope.

Suggested fix: reqwest::Client::builder().timeout(Duration::from_secs(30)).connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).build() — values appropriate for a secrets fetch (interactive latency budget).

Trigger: network partition mid-request.
Actual: future hangs.
Expected: SecretError::Unavailable with timeout source within bounded time.
title: No HTTP timeout on reqwest::Client; get/put may hang indefinitely
